title: Love Those Breasts

jokes.text: QVC offered me my own line of clothing... The only reason I didn't do it was... they're just gross, man. They just nasty people.
jokes.setup: Dave mentions QVC offered him a clothing line, creating expectation for why he turned it down
jokes.punchline: Instead of business reasons, he calls them 'gross' and 'nasty people'
jokes.explanation: Good setup that builds expectation then delivers unexpected petty reason

jokes.text: Uh, Ron, uh, I think you have a booger on your hand. You're right, Jessica, I do! Ha, ha, ha! Better get rid of it, before one of our customers bids on it! I'll start the bidding at $9.95. Sold!
jokes.setup: Normal QVC pitch gets interrupted by gross observation about booger
jokes.punchline: Instead of being embarrassed, they incorporate the booger into the sale
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from gross to absurd, perfectly captures QVC's shameless sales tactics

jokes.text: It's not genital herpes, it's just a cold sore! No, that's genital herpes. Believe me, I should know, I've had it since 1979.
jokes.setup: Model tries to downplay her cold sore as not genital herpes
jokes.punchline: Host corrects her and reveals his own decades-long infection
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation with specific detail (1979) that makes it more disturbing and funny

jokes.text: Whoa! It's a jungle down there. (Tarzan yell) Me Jane. She hairy. The rugs don't quite match the drapes, have you noticed that?
jokes.setup: Host commenting on model's bikini presentation
jokes.punchline: References excessive body hair with jungle metaphor and Tarzan impression
jokes.explanation: Decent visual comedy with multiple hair puns, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Most niggas would be like... Gotta do what I gotta do, son. We all have our faults, you know.
jokes.setup: Dave asks what you'd do if you saw that hairy lady
jokes.punchline: Impression of resigned acceptance despite being grossed out
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about male desperation with realistic character voice

jokes.text: Oh, oh! Oh, I'm... hey, I'm sorry. I told y'all not to give me a real baby. Pissin' all over me and (beep).
jokes.setup: Dramatic slave narrative scene with baby-holding moment
jokes.punchline: Actor breaks character to complain about real baby urinating on him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy breaking serious historical drama with absurd practical filmmaking problem

jokes.text: Damn! Steve, what'd I say about hitting so hard, man? I'm sorry, are you all right? You sorry? I'm gonna show you sorry, man! I'm gonna show you sorry!
jokes.setup: Whipping scene from Roots gets too real
jokes.punchline: Actor breaks character to complain, then threatens to fight back for real
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation from meta-complaint to actual threat of violence, subverting the power dynamic

jokes.text: I told y'all he's scared of me!
jokes.setup: Continuation of the behind-scenes fight
jokes.punchline: Actor boasts about scaring the 'overseer' actor
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the meta-joke about actors breaking character

jokes.text: by me having a white friend, like, 'how incredible, look at 'em, talking and laughing together.'
jokes.setup: Dave explaining why his first show Buddies failed
jokes.punchline: Mocks the premise of interracial friendship being inherently noteworthy
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary disguised as self-deprecation about tokenistic TV representation

jokes.text: Well, kids, a doctor's job is hard, and it never gets harder than telling you guys that your parents are dead.
jokes.setup: Kids expecting normal hidden camera prank
jokes.punchline: Dave delivers devastating fake news in cheerful doctor voice
jokes.explanation: Incredibly dark and original subversion of hidden camera genre with perfect casual delivery

jokes.text: Hopefully, they don't split you up. All right, take care. Oh, by the way, I forgot to tell you... Psych! I'm just kidding, come on out, mom and dad, come on out.
jokes.setup: Dave continues the fake death announcement with more cruel details
jokes.punchline: Casual 'Psych!' reveal after maximum psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and timing on the reveal, with casual cruelty that's both horrifying and hilarious

jokes.text: I ain't JJ, but it was dyn-o-mite!
jokes.setup: Woman's boyfriend cheating confession mentions 'JJ'
jokes.punchline: Dave corrects identity but keeps the Good Times catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Quick wordplay on pop culture reference, economical but not particularly original

jokes.text: It's a dildo, dawg!
jokes.setup: Couple fighting over apparent infidelity evidence
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'evidence' is just a sex toy
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection reveal with good economy, though the setup is somewhat obvious

jokes.text: I eat cottage cheese for dinner, all right? With salt and pepper. You understand?
jokes.setup: Bank teller mentions having a family during robbery
jokes.punchline: Robber counters with pathetic meal details to justify his desperation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd character detail that humanizes the robber in the most pathetic way possible

jokes.text: What, I can't make fun of that fucking frog? Fuck that frog. They don't be doing that on white networks. As soon as a black network... at the dubba WB.
jokes.setup: Dave apparently got in trouble for making fun of something
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's the WB frog mascot and calls out network double standards
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on media representation and corporate censorship with racial angle

jokes.text: Big boobs, bad back.
jokes.setup: Friend asks about Sheila's stretching
jokes.punchline: Simple cause-and-effect explanation with alliteration
jokes.explanation: Basic observational humor with decent alliteration but very predictable

jokes.text: Look at that lady. Yeah, her bosooms are, like, bouncin' around and everything.
jokes.setup: Boys watching Sheila jog
jokes.punchline: Innocent but obvious fascination with bouncing breasts
jokes.explanation: Simple character comedy with innocent kids noticing what adults would. 'Bosooms' is charming.

jokes.text: Stupid boobs. Nothing but an inconvenience. I wish I didn't have 'em!
jokes.setup: Sheila struggling with sports bra during exercise
jokes.punchline: Wishes away her breasts in frustration - sets up the whole sketch premise
jokes.explanation: Essential setup line that launches the entire 'It's a Wonderful Life' parody premise.

jokes.text: Careful what you wish for. What? Who are you? Who I am is not important, but what you said about those titties is.
jokes.setup: Mysterious figure appears after Sheila wishes away her breasts
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes titties over his own identity in overly serious tone
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between mysterious supernatural tone and crude obsession with breasts

jokes.text: What happened to my boobs? / Oh, you just answered your own question. Them boobies is gone, girl, just like you wished for.
jokes.setup: Sheila confused about changed treatment at work
jokes.punchline: Angel points out the obvious cause-and-effect
jokes.explanation: Good revelation moment that drives home the premise - clear cause and effect.

jokes.text: Them boobies is gone, girl, just like you wished for.
jokes.setup: Sheila notices her breasts are gone
jokes.punchline: Dave's casual street vernacular contrasts with his mystical role
jokes.explanation: Character comedy from mixing street language with supernatural guide role

jokes.text: In fact, tell that titless freak she's fired.
jokes.setup: Boss dismisses Sheila for promotion due to lack of breasts
jokes.punchline: Escalates from denial to firing with cruel terminology
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with memorable harsh phrase, though concept is predictable

jokes.text: Boo-yah!
jokes.setup: Sheila being fired for lacking breasts
jokes.punchline: Angel's inappropriate celebration of proving his point
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - angel inappropriately celebrating her misfortune to prove his point.

jokes.text: You know what you need? A new set of titties and a boyfriend, then you'll be all set, all right?
jokes.setup: Paul's honest assessment of their friendship
jokes.punchline: Brutally direct advice reducing her worth to physical attributes
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe humor with memorable blunt advice that summarizes the sketch's theme

jokes.text: Well, it's not what's gotten into him as much as it is what's gotten out of you. By that, of course, I mean your massive milk bladders.
jokes.setup: Sheila confused by Paul's changed behavior
jokes.punchline: Angel's elaborate euphemism for breasts
jokes.explanation: 'Massive milk bladders' is a brilliantly crude euphemism that fits the character perfectly.

jokes.text: He can't see you, but my black ass got 20/20 vision. God damn, Sheila!
jokes.setup: Sheila tries to prove she still has breasts to Paul
jokes.punchline: Dave breaks mystical character to admire her body with street vernacular
jokes.explanation: Great character break moment with quotable line mixing mystical premise with crude appreciation

jokes.text: am I getting bridesmaids, or starting an itty-bitty titty committee?
jokes.setup: Bride explaining why she didn't invite Sheila to wedding
jokes.punchline: Alliterative phrase reducing bridesmaids to breast size qualification
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with strong alliteration, though somewhat predictable in this context

jokes.text: 'Cause your friends are bitches! Besides, Sheila, even girls love girls with huge tats, especially at a wedding. The groom's friends gotta have somebody to sleep with.
jokes.setup: Sheila shocked by her friends' behavior
jokes.punchline: Angel's crude but honest assessment of wedding dynamics
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest character moment that reveals the angel's crude wisdom about human nature.

jokes.text: Besides, Sheila, even girls love girls with huge tats, especially at a wedding. The groom's friends gotta have somebody to sleep with.
jokes.setup: Dave explaining why women also prefer women with large breasts
jokes.punchline: Reduces wedding dynamics to crude sexual marketplace
jokes.explanation: Cynical but believable observation delivered with characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Listen, I didn't want to put this on you, but the fate of the world... you hear me... the world rests on them chesticles!
jokes.setup: Building up the importance of her breasts
jokes.punchline: Escalates to world-ending consequences depending on 'chesticles'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to absurdist heights. 'Chesticles' is brilliantly crafted word creation.

jokes.text: Listen, I didn't want to put this on you, but the fate of the world... you hear me... the world rests on them chesticles!
jokes.setup: Dave builds up to ultimate consequence of breast removal
jokes.punchline: Global apocalypse depends on one woman's breasts, delivered with made-up word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to absurd stakes with quotable made-up word 'chesticles'

jokes.text: masturbating to you and those giant cans of yours was all he had to live for. Once you took that away, he lost his mind and blew up the world!
jokes.setup: Dave explains the neighbor's motivation for world destruction
jokes.punchline: Global annihilation caused by removal of masturbation material
jokes.explanation: Dark and original causal chain linking personal sexual frustration to global destruction

jokes.text: World's going to end in about 10 seconds... which is just enough time to suck a titty. But where would I find a titty from? I don't... well, hey, it's Sheila!
jokes.setup: Angel lamenting the world's end
jokes.punchline: Tries to use the last moments for personal gratification
jokes.explanation: Perfect character consistency - even at the apocalypse, he's still thinking about breasts.

jokes.text: World's going to end in about 10 seconds... which is just enough time to suck a titty. But where would I find a titty from? I don't... well, hey, it's Sheila!
jokes.setup: Dave laments lack of time and breasts as world ends
jokes.punchline: Conveniently 'notices' Sheila has her breasts back just in time
jokes.explanation: Good setup and payoff for character's priorities, though resolution is somewhat convenient

jokes.text: I've got humongoid knockers, and I'm proud of them.
jokes.setup: Sheila's final declaration of self-acceptance
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'humongoid' combines humongous and mammary reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay though character transformation feels somewhat unearned

jokes.text: Angel? I ain't no angel, I'm a janitor. Girl, I am high on PCP.
jokes.setup: Sheila assumes Dave is an angel for showing her visions
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's just a drug-addled janitor
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection reveal that recontextualizes entire sketch - brilliant subversion of It's a Wonderful Life trope

jokes.text: I was gonna ask you how you was following me. You smoke sherm... a little sherman?
jokes.setup: Dave continues as confused drug user
jokes.punchline: Tries to offer PCP to the woman he just helped
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that maintains the drug user character while offering to share

jokes.text: I'm just a nigga that love titties.
jokes.setup: Sheila asks who he is after the revelation
jokes.punchline: Simple, honest self-description that reduces entire cosmic premise to basic motivation
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of character and sketch theme with maximum economy and honesty

jokes.text: I'm rich, bi-atch!
jokes.setup: Dave ending the show
jokes.punchline: Delivers his signature catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Dave catchphrase delivery - not original to this episode but always effective.

jokes.text: WB? Man, you told me this (bleep) was for HBO, man. Ain't dying for no damn WB.
jokes.setup: Behind-scenes moment during filming
jokes.punchline: Dave's standards for which network is worth risking injury for
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on network hierarchy and actor dedication based on prestige

jokes.text: I had some titties on my neck.
jokes.setup: Dave apologizes for not listening during previous scene
jokes.punchline: Explains distraction with bizarre physical detail
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse with good economy, though somewhat random

tmdb_overview: Dave plays an angel who shows a woman, regretful of her large chest, the error of her ways in "It's a Wonderful Chest." A shopping network spoof finds QVC selling very odd and distasteful items. Dave also shares humorous outtakes from the classic miniseries "Roots." Also featuring musical performer Talib Kweli.
